ProductDocsPricingCompare
Star on GitHubGet started

Compare

Recurso vs Lago

Both are open-source billing engines you can self-host for zero software fees. The differences that matter are the license (MIT vs AGPLv3), the breadth of the billing surface, and India-native compliance. Lago is strong at usage metering; Recurso is broader and India-first.

Start self-hostingRead the quickstart

MIT licensed · Both self-hostable · Stripe + Razorpay + UPI AutoPay

Why Recurso

What Recurso brings

MIT vs AGPLv3

Recurso is MIT licensed — permissive enough to fork, embed, or ship inside closed-source products. Lago is AGPLv3, a network copyleft license that many corporate legal teams restrict.

India-native compliance

Recurso builds in GST and e-invoicing for Indian businesses. Lago does not list India tax compliance as a focus.

Multi-gateway payments

Recurso ships Stripe, Razorpay, and UPI AutoPay out of the box for both global and Indian collections.

Double-entry ledger

Every invoice and payment in Recurso posts to a double-entry ledger (TigerBeetle-backed). Lago is strongest at high-volume usage metering.

Capabilities

Recurso vs Lago, feature by feature

Capability
Recurso
Lago
Self-hosted option
MIT-licensed
No platform fees
Usage-based billing
Full subscription lifecycle
Multi-gateway (Stripe + Razorpay)
UPI AutoPay
India GST & e-invoicing
Double-entry ledger
Full support Partial Not available
vs FlexPricevs Kill Billvs Chargebeevs Stripe Billing

Start owning your billing tonight

One command from clone to a seeded dashboard. Free forever on your own infrastructure — or get in line for Recurso Cloud.

Start self-hosting

git clone https://github.com/swapnull-in/recur-so.git && make demo

recurso

The open-source billing engine for SaaS. Built with Go, PostgreSQL, and TigerBeetle. MIT licensed.
